prompt:
Commerce-platform ticket. Package order (order.go, attached — in production, works) walks the happy path cart -> placed -> paid -> shipped -> delivered off an explicit transition table, with guards and an append-only history. Finance signed off on compensation flows last sprint and now I have to add them. The EXISTING BEHAVIOR block in order_test.go passes against the shipped code and must stay green, byte-identical semantics; the COMPENSATION, ROLE GATE and HISTORY INVARIANT blocks are the new contract. `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...` fully green is done; test file read-only.

Seed-ledger note: nearest neighbors are ts-approval-flow (amount-tier approval ROUTING for expense reports) and go-supervisor (infra restart-policy decision core) — this seed is neither routing nor restart policy: it is a goods-and-money lifecycle with compensation legs and ledger-style history invariants. go-circuit-breaker is not re-skinned (no thresholds, no clock).

What finance specified:

1. Compensation states and events. New states StateCancelled="cancelled", StateReturned="returned", StateClosed="closed"; new events EventCancel="cancel", EventReturn="return", EventRefund="refund". Table additions: placed --cancel--> closed (nothing was captured, close directly); paid --cancel--> cancelled; delivered --return--> returned; cancelled --refund--> closed; returned --refund--> closed. Everything else stays illegal and keeps reporting TransitionError with From and Event filled in — cancel from cart/shipped/delivered, return from anywhere but delivered, refund straight from paid.

2. Role-gated cancel. type Role string with RoleCustomer="customer", RoleAgent="agent". Cancel(role Role) error: from placed either role may cancel; from paid ONLY an agent may (customers call support once money moved). Return(role Role) error: either role, from delivered. Any role that isn't one of the two constants is rejected. Refusals are PermissionError{Role, Event} (pointer errors.As-able, message naming both). Check order is pinned: transition-table legality first (TransitionError), THEN role (PermissionError) — an unknown role poking an illegal state must read as the state problem. A refused call changes nothing: state and history untouched.

3. Refund(amount int) error plus Refunded() int. Refund is guarded like Pay: the amount must equal the outstanding captured money (Paid() - Refunded()) exactly, else GuardError with Event=EventRefund and no state change. A successful refund records Entry{EventRefund, from, StateClosed, amount} and closes the order.

4. History invariants. We are importing order histories from the legacy system, so expose the checker standalone: AuditHistory(entries []Entry, final State) error, plus convenience (*Order).Audit() error = AuditHistory(History(), State()). Rules — violations return InvariantError (struct, errors.As-able, reason in the message): (a) an empty history is valid only when final is cart; (b) the first entry must leave cart; (c) entries must chain, each From equal to the previous To; (d) the last entry's To must equal final; (e) only pay and refund entries carry an Amount and it must be positive there, zero everywhere else; (f) at every prefix of the history, cumulative refunds must never exceed cumulative payments; (g) if final is StateClosed the money must balance: total refunds == total payments. AuditHistory judges chain and money rules only — it does NOT re-check table legality, because legacy flows used routes the current table no longer has.

Keep it a pure decision core (no clocks, no I/O), keep the transition table the single source of which event is legal where, and don't break a single existing test.
